questions.question: Which of the following are the core features of the Express framework?
questions.answer: All of the above.
questions.explanation: Express.js provides: (1) Middleware support for HTTP requests, (2) Routing table based on HTTP method and URL, and (3) Dynamic HTML rendering. All three are core features of Express.
options: It allows us to set up middleware to respond to HTTP Requests.
It defines a routing table that can work as per HTTP Method and URL.
It is used to render the HTML pages dynamically.

questions.question: Where are the captured values populated regarding the route parameters?
questions.answer: req.params
questions.explanation: In Express.js, route parameters (defined with :paramName) are captured and stored in the req.params object. For example, for route '/user/:id', req.params.id holds the value.

questions.question: Which of the following function arguments are available to Express.js Route handlers?
questions.answer: All of the above
questions.explanation: Express route handlers receive three arguments: req (request object), res (response object), and next (function to pass control to the next middleware). All three are available.

questions.question: Which of the following is a middleware in Express.js?
questions.answer: function(req,res,next){ }
questions.explanation: Middleware in Express is a function with three parameters: req, res, and next. The next parameter differentiates middleware from regular route handlers. Calling next() passes control to the next middleware.

questions.question: Which of the following function is used to specify what to do when a GET request at the given route is called?
questions.answer: app.get(route, callback)
questions.explanation: app.get(route, callback) in Express registers a route handler for GET requests. 'app' is the Express application object. get() and js.get() are not valid.

questions.question: Which of the following HTTP method, are used to transmit a limited amount of data, as the data is included in the header?
questions.answer: GET
questions.explanation: GET requests send data as query parameters in the URL (headers/URL), which has a size limit. POST sends data in the request body, supporting larger amounts. GET is visible in the URL.

questions.question: ____ requests are used to transmit a large amount of data, as the data is included in the body of the request?
questions.answer: POST
questions.explanation: POST requests include data in the request body, which has no size limit. This makes POST suitable for large data like form submissions, file uploads, and JSON payloads.

questions.question: ____ functions are those that interact with the request and response objects (req, res) during the request-response cycle?
questions.answer: Middleware
questions.explanation: Middleware functions in Express have access to req, res, and next objects. They can modify the request/response, end the cycle, or call next() to pass control to the next middleware.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Express.js app.listen() function?
questions.answer: To start the application server
questions.explanation: app.listen(port, callback) starts the Express server and makes it listen for incoming connections on the specified port. The callback is called when the server starts successfully.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the req object in Express.js?
questions.answer: To represent the HTTP request
questions.explanation: The req (request) object represents the incoming HTTP request and contains properties for query string, parameters, body, HTTP headers, cookies, etc.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the res object in Express.js?
questions.answer: To represent the HTTP response
questions.explanation: The res (response) object represents the HTTP response that an Express app sends when it gets an HTTP request. It has methods like res.send(), res.json(), res.render(), res.redirect().

questions.question: _____ method is used to set content type in express JS. Consider res, app and expr as objects of response, express alias and express respectively.
questions.answer: res.set()
questions.explanation: res.set() (or res.setHeader()) is used to set response headers including Content-Type. For example: res.set('content-type', 'text/html'). app.set() sets application-level settings.

questions.question: Identify the valid signature of app.get() method. req, res and next are the objects of request, response and middleware respectively.
questions.answer: app.get("/",function(req,res,next){});
questions.explanation: app.get() in Express accepts the route path and a callback. The callback can have (req), (req,res), or (req,res,next). All variations are valid. The question asks for the valid signature including next.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Express.js res.redirect() method?
questions.answer: To redirect the client to a different URL
questions.explanation: res.redirect(url) in Express sends a redirect response (3xx status) that tells the browser to navigate to a different URL. For example: res.redirect('/home') redirects to the /home route.
options: To send data back to the client
To render a view template in the application
To redirect the client to a different URL
To handle errors that occur in the application

questions.question: What is to be used to display captured values route parameters?
questions.answer: req.params
questions.explanation: req.params contains properties mapped to named route parameters. For route '/users/:id', accessing req.params.id gives the value in the URL. req.query is for query strings.

questions.question: What is the purpose of response.render() method in express.js?
questions.answer: to send response back to client with a rendered template
questions.explanation: res.render(viewName, data) renders a template file (like EJS, Pug) with the provided data and sends the resulting HTML to the client. It requires a view engine to be configured.
options: to send response back to client with a rendered template
to update database record based on data submitted in form
to redirect client to different URL
to parse incoming request bodies and extract form data

questions.question:
Identify output/error from following code. uname is a name of textfield having content 'xyz'. response.set("content-type","application/json") response.send(`

${request.body.uname}

`)
questions.answer: xyz
questions.explanation:
Even though content-type is application/json, res.send() can send any string. The browser will display '

xyz

' as plain text (not rendered as HTML) because the content-type is JSON. But the string value is

xyz

.
options: Error as content inside send() is not a JSON object

questions.answer: Will give html output
questions.explanation: res.send() in Express automatically detects the string type and sets Content-Type to text/html when an HTML string is passed. The browser will render the HTML, showing the heading and paragraph.
options: Error : html can not be defined as variable
Error : html can not be passed directly to res.send()
Error : html tags should be written in one line

questions.question: What is the purpose of req.params object in Express.js?
questions.answer: To store HTTP request parameters
questions.explanation: req.params is an object containing properties mapped to named route parameters (:paramName). For route '/product/:id', req.params.id contains the value passed in the URL.

questions.answer: red-colored saying "welcome everyone"
questions.explanation:
Content-Type is text/html, so the browser renders the HTML. The

tag with color:red styles the text in red. The output is 'welcome everyone' in red color (not big font, as no font-size is specified).

questions.answer: Nothing gets print (Blank page) with error in console: Cannot set headers after they are sent to the client
questions.explanation: res.write() sends 'Hello world!' and internally starts sending the response. Then res.send() tries to set new headers and send more data, but headers are already sent, causing 'Cannot set headers after they are sent to the client' error.
options: Nothing gets print (Blank page) with error in console: Cannot set headers after they are sent to the client

questions.answer: Nothing gets print (Blank page)
questions.explanation: res.write('Hello') sends a chunk but res.json() then tries to set Content-Type: application/json and write more. This conflicts with the already-started response. Similar to the previous question, a 'Cannot set headers' error occurs.

questions.question: Identify the output of following Express code that sorts students by age and uses res.write() for each. students = [{name:'ABC',age:28},{name:'PQR',age:31},{name:'XYZ',age:20}]
questions.answer:
ABC 20
PQR 28
XYZ 31
questions.explanation: The bubble sort swaps ages but NOT names. So ages become sorted [20,28,31] but names stay [ABC,PQR,XYZ]. Final output: ABC 20, PQR 28, XYZ 31 — names in original order with sorted ages.

questions.answer: FirstThird
questions.explanation: app.use('/test', cb) registers only cb as middleware (not cb1). When /test is requested: cb writes 'First' and calls next(), which passes to the route handler writing 'Third'. cb1 is never registered so 'Second' never prints. Content-Type is text/html so tags render.

questions.question: What does the following do, app.listen(port, [callback]])?
questions.answer: This function binds and waits for connections on the provided host.
questions.explanation: app.listen() starts an HTTP server and binds it to the specified port, waiting for incoming client connections. The optional callback is called when the server is ready.
options: This method accepts an object as input and returns it to the requesting client.
This method specifies what to execute when a get request at the specified route is made.
This method is used to transmit a limited amount of data.
This function binds and waits for connections on the provided host.

questions.question: Write an express.js script to load an HTML file having username and password and submit button. On clicking submit button it should jump on 'check' page using POST method. If username is 'admin', print 'welcome... admin'; if username is not 'admin', print 'warning msg' in red color.
questions.explanation: express.urlencoded() middleware parses POST body data. The checkAdmin middleware checks username — if 'admin', calls next() to proceed; otherwise sends error in red. The route handler only executes if middleware calls next().

questions.question: Write an express.js script to print 'hello World' also run on localhost:4000
questions.explanation: The simplest Express app: require express, create app instance, define a GET route for '/', send 'hello World' response, listen on port 4000.

questions.question: Write an express.js script to use routing method using home, about, contact, temp page and print message. /home - welcome to my home page, /about - welcome to my about page, /contact - welcome to my contact page, /temp - welcome to my temp page
questions.explanation: Each route is defined with app.get(). Express routing is cleaner than Node's if-else approach. app.get('*') catches all unmatched routes as a 404 handler.

questions.question: Write an express.js script to make one index.html file with a simple heading tag, make one CSS file index.css with style using appropriate selector, link with a JS file and show output on localhost:3030.
questions.explanation: express.static() serves static files (HTML, CSS, JS, images) from a directory. The path module ensures cross-platform compatible paths. Files in 'public' folder are served automatically.

questions.question: Write an express.js to implement a form that allows users to submit data via POST request. Write code to create a route that handles the POST request and logs the submitted data to the console.
questions.explanation: express.urlencoded() is required to parse form data from POST requests. req.body contains the form fields. console.log logs the data. Destructuring extracts username and email from req.body.

questions.question: How can you create and use a middleware function in express js?
questions.explanation: Middleware are functions that run before route handlers. They can log, authenticate, parse data, etc. next() is crucial — without it, the request hangs. Order of app.use() calls determines execution order.

questions.question: Write an express js to link html, css and js file and show one image o.jpg. And also show the description of image.
questions.explanation: All static files (HTML, CSS, JS, images) go inside the 'public' folder. express.static() serves them automatically. The HTML links to CSS, includes JS, shows the image with a description paragraph.

questions.question: Using Express JS make student information form with only two fields like name and mobile number and send data on console after submitting 'submit' button.
questions.explanation: This Express.js app serves an HTML form via GET, and handles the POST submission by extracting name and mobile from req.body and logging them to the console.

questions.question: Write an ExpressJS to take a textarea and submit button. After clicking submit button the content of textarea should be represented on next page by writing each sentence (separated by dot) in new line.
questions.explanation:
The textarea content is split by '.' (dot), each sentence is trimmed and displayed on a new line using

tags on the result page.

questions.question: Write an expressJS code which loads login.html file upon browsing localhost:3010. The login.html file contains input for username, password and two checkboxes named remember and subscribe. On submitting it should go to /data page where username, password and selected checkboxes are printed. Additionally a logout button should be there, on clicking it should go back to home page 'localhost:3010/'. (Write all necessary files code)
questions.explanation: Two files are needed: login.html for the form, and app.js which serves that file on GET '/' and processes the POST data on '/data'. Logout is a GET redirect back to '/'.

questions.question: Write an express.js script to define one JSON array of 3 objects having members 'name' and 'height'. Height must be sorted in descending order according to name.
questions.explanation: An array of 3 objects each with name and height is defined, sorted descending by height using Array.sort() with a comparator, and displayed on the browser.

questions.question: Write an express.js script to define one JSON array of 3 objects having members 'name' and 'score'. Score must be sorted in descending order according to name.
questions.explanation: Array of 3 player objects sorted by score in descending order using sort() with comparator b.score - a.score.

questions.question: Write an Express JS script to link html, css and js file to show message 'LJ University with a difference' with font size 50px and color 'blue'.
questions.explanation: express.static() serves static files from the 'public' folder. index.html links to the CSS and JS files. CSS sets font-size and color; JS injects the text.

questions.question: Write an ExpressJS to take a UserName, Password, Textarea for 'message' and submit button using GET method. 1) After clicking submit, submitted details should be represented on '/login' page along with one 'show vowel' link. 2) By clicking 'show vowel' link count of vowels used in submitted 'message' will display on '/message' page. (Use next() to route page)
questions.explanation: GET form submits to /login which displays user details and a link. /message route counts vowels in the message using regex /[aeiouAEIOU]/g.

questions.question: Write an express.js script to print 'The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest of the world ocean' also run on appropriate localhost.
questions.explanation: A basic Express app that responds to GET '/' with the required sentence wrapped in an h1 tag, running on port 3000.

questions.question: Write expressJS script to perform tasks: (A) Create one HTML file with text-field named username, dropdown with country options (India, USA, Canada, Australia) and one submit button. (B) Once user clicks submit it jumps to next page and displays username & 'You are from <country name>'. (C) Use GET method.
questions.explanation: The HTML form uses GET method to submit username and country to /result route where the values are read from req.query and displayed.

questions.question: What is middleware in Express JS? And how is it used in handling HTTP requests?
questions.explanation: Middleware functions sit between the incoming request and the final route handler. Calling next() passes control to the next middleware or route.

questions.question: How to link HTML, CSS and JS file in Express JS? Give any short example for it.
questions.explanation: express.static('public') makes Express serve all files in the 'public' folder. The HTML file then links CSS and JS with relative paths.

questions.question: Write an ExpressJS application that creates a login form containing fields for username and password along with a submit button. The form data should be sent to the server using the POST method. Implement an authentication middleware that checks whether the entered username is 'admin' and the password is '1234'. If credentials are correct, display 'Login Successful'. Otherwise display 'Invalid Credentials'.
questions.explanation: authMiddleware checks credentials. If valid, next() is called to proceed to the success handler. Otherwise, it directly sends 'Invalid Credentials' without calling next().

questions.question: Write an ExpressJS application that creates an HTML form containing two text fields named name and age along with a submit button. When user submits the form, data should be sent using GET method. After submission, redirect to a new page and display: 'Hello <name>, your age is <age>'.
questions.explanation: Form uses GET so values go to req.query. /greet redirects to /result which displays the final message using template literals.

questions.question: How to send HTML and JSON data as a Response using Express JS.
questions.explanation: res.send() handles HTML, res.json() handles JSON. The Content-Type header differs: text/html vs application/json.
